channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Pegula Ice Arena hosting Frozen Four watch party
item.description: UNIVERSITY PARK (Nittany Nation) -- Penn State athletics announced a Frozen Four watch party Thursday night at the Pegula Ice arena. Penn State hockey plays Boston University Thursday at 8:30 p.m. in the Nittany Lions' first Frozen Four appearance.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Clay, PHL 17

item.title: Bulls championship banners back up in United Center rafters earlier than expected
item.description: The Chicago Bulls' six championship banners have been rehung at the United Center after being removed due to damage from a rock concert by Disturbed.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: MLB batting average drops again to .239, near record low in 1968's Year of the Pitcher
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Offense is down again at the start of the big league season, with hits even harder to come by. The big league batting average was .239 through the first full week of play, down from .240 through the first full week of 2024.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Red Sox's Jarren Duran opens up about mental health, attempted suicide in Netflix documentary
item.description: Red Sox outfielder Jarren Duran said in an upcoming documentary series that two years before his 2024 All-Star season, the pressures of the sport led him to intense bouts of depression and an attempted suicide.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Hightower, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Maine sues Trump admin over funding freeze for refusal to keep trans athletes out of women's sports
item.description: Maine filed a lawsuit against the U.S. Department of Agriculture over a funding freeze the agency levied on the state for its refusal to alter its participation policy in sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panthers sign Colin Granger, ex-college basketball center, in attempt to make him tight end
item.description: Colin Granger, an ex-college basketball center, has signed with the Carolina Panthers, who will attempt to convert him into an NFL-caliber tight end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: March Madness wraps in Florida-Houston matchup pitting tourney's best defense vs best clutch player
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The national title game between Florida and Houston could come down to the NCAA Tournament's best clutch player against the country's best clutch defense. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One combined Final Four? Talks of a joint NCAA Tournament showcase for the men and women resurface
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Millions tuned in to watch women's basketball powerhouses UConn and South Carolina dominate in the Final Four on Friday. Many more watched Houston and Florida stun their opponents in the men's Final Four a day later.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UConn finishes season at No. 1 in AP women's basketball poll for first time since 2021
item.description: UConn capped its season at No. 1 in The Associated Press Top 25 women’s basketball pol l on Monday, one day after winning its 12th national championship. The Huskies received all 31 votes from a national media panel after Geno Auriemma's team beat South Carolina 82-59 for the title.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Practice is suspended at the Masters because of rain and a threat of severe storms
item.description: Practice at the Masters was suspended late Monday morning and spectators were pulled off the golf course because of worsening rain and the threat of thunderstorms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: McLaren can be beaten, but Verstappen must be perfect: 5 takeaways from F1’s Japanese Grand Prix
item.description: McLaren remains the team to beat in Formula 1, but Max Verstappen’s victorious drive for Red Bull at the Japanese Grand Prix showed only excellence is good enough. Ahead of this week’s Bahrain Grand Prix, here are five takeaways from Suzuka: So, McLaren can be beaten.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vladimir Guerrero Jr, Blue Jays agree to massive 14-year contract extension: reports
item.description: Vladimir Guerrero Jr. and the Toronto Blue Jays reportedly agreed to a massive 14-year contract extension worth up to $500 million early Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Women's NCAA Final Four tournament a 'slam dunk for Tampa's economy
item.description: Even though the women’s championship is over, Tampa businesses are calling this weekend a "slam dunk." This is the fourth time the city has hosted the NCAA women’s basketball Final Four, and it’s not just basketball fans who are winning.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A healthy Azzi Fudd stars as UConn wins women's NCAA Tournament
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — This time, Azzi Fudd was healthy. And she made a big difference for UConn. Fudd was named the Most Outstanding Player of the Final Four after leading the Huskies to the program's 12th national championship.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After 149 wins over 6 seasons, J'Wan Roberts needs 1 more in last game for Houston's 1st NCAA title
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — J'Wan Roberts has been at Houston for six seasons and played in 149 wins for the Cougars. Win one more time in his final game Monday night against Florida, and the Cougars will be first-time national champions. “It feels great.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UConn coach Geno Auriemma's 3-word message to Paige Bueckers in emotional embrace after winning championship
item.description: Paige Bueckers and Geno Auriemma shared an emotional embrace as she came off the court as a UConn Husky for the final time, which ended in a national championship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UConn returns to top of women's basketball, dominates South Carolina 82-59 to capture its 12th title
item.description: Dawn Staley and South Carolina hope to finish off a second straight season with an NCAA championship, which would give the Gamecocks a third title in four years. To do so, they'll need to beat Paige Bueckers and UConn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred breaks silence on torpedo bats
item.description: MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred expressed support for the torpedo bats in a recent interview amid the craze that's been the talk of baseball.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record by scoring his 895th
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Ovechkin has broken Wayne Gretzky’s record for the most goals in NHL history by scoring the 895th of his career. The Washington Capitals’ captain made history Sunday against the New York Islanders by beating fellow Russian Ilya Sorokin on a power play in the second period.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carpenter and Torkelson homer as Tigers beat White Sox 7-2 and drop Chicago to 2-6
item.description: Kerry Carpenter homered for the third time in two games and Spencer Torkelson had a home run and a double Saturday as the Detroit Tigers beat the Chicago White Sox 7-2 for their fourth win in five games following an opening three-game losing streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bulls Head Coach Billy Donovan joins Carmelo Anthony, Sue Bird and others in Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, WGN 9

item.title: Capitals beat Blackhawks 5-3, Ovechkin scores two goals to tie Gretzky
item.description: Alex Ovechkin tied Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record by scoring the 893rd and 894th of his career, the second being the game winner, as the Washington Capitals rallied to beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-3 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kings score three times in third period to beat Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club's playoff chances are growing slimmer with each loss, and it looks like it's going to take a miraculous turn of events to get in now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Two De Beque Dragons sign to college: Ava Vines and Allyson Sandidge
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Despite having a population of less than 500, despite having just a small 1A school, De Beque, Colorado, and the De Beque Dragons have had a lineage of college athletes come through their program.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: BYU guard Dallin Hall enters transfer portal
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU point guard announced on Wednesday that he is entering the transfer portal after three seasons with the Cougars.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jon Scheyer, a Chicagoland prep basketball legend, going for 3rd national title with Duke
item.description: Jon Scheyer is two victories shy of winning his third NCAA Division I national championship with the storied Duke men's basketball program: one as a player, one as an assistant coach, and — with wins in San Antonio on Saturday and Monday — one as a head coach.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs, Seiya Suzuki look to stay sizzling when they return to Wrigley Field
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Pull out the hand warmers. Seiya Suzuki is headed home to Wrigley Field at last, and he promises to be well prepared for whatever chilly elements he faces in an unpredictable Chicago spring.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Utes ready to rock the Regionals
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah gymnastics team is the only program in the nation that has qualified for all 48 NCAA National Championship gymnastics meets.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Miranda gets big hit as the Twins beat the White Sox 8-3 for their first win this season
item.description: Jose Miranda hit a tiebreaking two-run single in Minnesota's five-run sixth inning, and the Twins beat the Chicago White Sox 8-3 on Tuesday night for their first win of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Curry hits 12 3-pointers, scores 52 as Warriors defeat Grizzlies 134-125
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Stephen Curry had 52 points, including 12 3-pointers, as the Golden State Warriors held on for a 134-125 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Investigation into death of former Yankees player's son turns to carbon monoxide: reports
item.description: Investigators in Costa Rica say carbon monoxide may instead be to blame for the death of Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-Yankee player's son may have died of carbon monoxide poisoning
item.description: Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner, may have died from abnormal levels of carbon monoxide.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: A's routed by Cubs 18-3 in Sacramento home opener
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) -- Carson Kelly hit for the cycle and drove in five runs, Michael Busch launched a two-run homer on the way to a career-high four RBIs, and the Chicago Cubs pounded the Athletics 18-3 on Monday night to spoil their first game in their new Northern California home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Thunder rout Bulls 145-117 for 10th straight win, improve to 28-1 vs the East
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 27 points and 12 assists in just 27 minutes, and the Oklahoma City Thunder rolled to a 145-117 victory over the Chicago Bulls on Monday night for their 10th straight victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz clinch worst record in franchise history
item.description: CHARLOTTE (ABC4 Sports) - It's official. The 2024-25 Utah Jazz will have the worst record in franchise history. Miles Bridges had 26 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists and the Charlotte Hornets defeated the Jazz 110-106 on Monday night hand Utah is 60th loss of the season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NFL owners meetings kick off in South Florida
item.description: With free agency still in full swing and the NFL Draft less than a month away, NFL owners, head coaches, and front office executives have made their way to The Breakers in Palm Beach for the league's annual owners meetings.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC
